headline: Strait of Malacca Piracy Incidents Rise to 85 in 2025
context: IMB recorded 85 piracy incidents in Strait of Malacca in 2025 highest since 2015.

cards.big: Malacca Strait piracy hit 85 incidents in 2025 highest in a decade
cards.sub: Attacks threaten world busiest shipping lane carrying 40% of global trade

cards.big: 68% involved armed robbery while boarding vessels at anchor or slow speed
cards.sub: Stolen items typically include cash crew belongings and ship equipment

cards.big: Trilateral patrol covers only 60% of the shipping lane
cards.sub: Night patrols reduced 25% due to fuel costs and vessel availability

cards.big: Insurance premiums for transit increased 15% affecting RM12 billion cargo
cards.sub: Lloyd reclassified 2 zones as elevated risk first time since 2016

cards.big: Piracy increase tests whether multilateral patrols can secure global shipping chokepoints
85 Malacca Strait piracy incidents in 2025. Maritime chokepoint security requires trilateral coordination.
